fields.description:
✨ ONE PLATFORM, A WHOLE WORLD OF OPPORTUNITY

The best jobs are often restricted to a few wealthy cities, but talent exists everywhere. Oyster set out to change this by launching a global employment platform that enables companies to hire, pay, and support talent anywhere.

We’re proof that a strong, engaged culture doesn't require an office. Since January 2020, Oyster has:

🌏 Built a distributed team of 400+ across 60+ countries

👩‍💻 Featured in Forbes https://www.forbes.com/sites/laurabegleybloom/2024/01/25/work-from-home-or-anywhere-top-30-companies-for-remote-jobs-in-2024/ as #9 in FlexJobs Top 30 Companies for remote jobs

🌈 Formed a diverse leadership team and a workforce that’s 60% female

🏆 Earned top employee engagement scores

🦄 Raised $286 million; valued at $1.2 billion

💚 Achieved B Corp status in 2023

Our momentum shows the power of global employment, and we’re just getting started! If you want to help us drive change and work remotely, we’d love for you to apply!

Location: While this position is posted in a specific location, all of Oyster’s positions are fully remote and you can work from home. Forever. To create the best experience for our new hire, this role requires you to be based within +3 / -3 UTC.

Role Summary

We’re looking to hire a Senior Manager, People Programs to play a pivotal role in delivering programs central to delivering a People Experience that inspires and motivates employees, drives Oyster towards its goals, fosters culture aligned with our values, and ensures ethical and responsible operations. A strong people experience is essential for Oyster’s success across a diverse and complex landscape.

The Senior Manager, People Programs will lead the development and delivery of our core People Programs working with your team of People Experience Generalists and Specialists to deliver programs in line with our company goals. You will oversee our employee experience, strategic programs, people data insights, and performance programs. Additionally, you will champion best practices and collaborate with the wider People team, Finance, Legal and other partners across the company to refine and develop the employee experience.

As a data-driven builder and an innovator, you are unafraid to try new things, build new processes, consistently innovate existing ones, and test creative new strategies. You understand when specific processes no longer serve as intended and require iteration. You thrive on details and operational excellence and hold a high bar for yourself, your peers, and your team.

This role will report to the Director, People Experience and lead our People Programs team.

Key Responsibilities

 - Develop and execute a comprehensive People Programs strategy that propels high-performance culture, takes the employee experience to new heights and maintains cultural cohesion.

 - Manage the development and communications of People Programs including onboarding, offboarding, compensation and reward, and performance management and others as aligned with company goals.

 - Overall responsibility for people operations strategy and management of the People Operations and Programs team.

 - Iterate and advance our People Operations capacity model to refine and mature our integration with the Oyster platform and operations.

 - Oversee the development and delivery of internal communication strategies that aligns with Oyster’s goals and support our globally distributed teams, including central company communication campaigns, senior leadership communications, and change communications.

 - Identify areas for improvement in existing People Programs and design new initiatives to address gaps and ensure our programs scale and grow with our business.

 - Oversee and lead delivery of employee engagement programs, strategic programs, people data insights, enablement strategy, communications strategy, and performance programs.

 - Measure the impact and effectiveness of People Programs through data analysis and reporting, and continuously iterate and improve based on feedback and results.

 - Drive diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts by developing and implementing strategies that create an inclusive and equitable work environment. Champion initiatives that support underrepresented groups and ensure fair and unbiased people practices across the organisation

 - Oversee company All Hands production ensuring it’s an engaging and informative experience that keeps employees up to date on company goals, key achievements, and highlights work aligned with our mission and values.

 - Partner closely with cross-functional teams across Oyster including Talent Acquisition, FP&A, Legal and Compliance.

 - Stay up to date with industry best practices and trends in People Programs, employee engagement, and high-performance cultures, and apply them to our organisation as appropriate.

Core Requirements

 - Proven experience designing and scaling People Programs in a fast-growing, global environment.

 - Adept at balancing standardized global programs with the specific cultural, legal, and operational nuances of multiple international jurisdictions.

 - A passion for building high-performance cultures that balance productivity with employee well-being and company values.

 - Strong analytical capability to deliver complex people analysis and reporting; specifically translating data insights into actionable strategies and measuring the long-term impact of people programs.

 - Experience acting as the leader overseeing company-wide messaging, including executive communications, change management communications, and company event production.

 - Experience designing and leading global enablement programs that provide managers and employees with the tools, training, and resources needed to succeed in their roles.

 - Proven ability to increase operational efficiency across people programs by identifying opportunities for automation and leveraging AI tools to streamline People Programs and administrative workflows.

 - Familiarity with global compensation structures, benefits, and reward recognition programs.

 - Strong project management skills with the ability to oversee multiple high-stakes workstreams simultaneously.

 - [BONUS] Experience working with project management, documentation and collaboration tools such as Asana, Notion, Loom and Miro, Gsuite, Slack.

 - [BONUS] Experience working within a globally distributed company

 - [BONUS] Experience with our core People Tech Stack: Hibob (HRIS), Ashby (ATS) and Sana (LMS).

You'll also need

 - A reliable home internet connection and fluency in both written and spoken English


HOW WE WORK TOGETHER AT OYSTER

Our values guide the work we do, the decisions we make, and the culture that makes us special. We make it happen. We drive change. We build and give trust, and we are a united team.

Our mission is to create a more equal world, one global hire at a time. Everything we do ladders up to our mission, and that doesn’t just mean building software. We develop programs, participate in workshops, and create dedicated teams to ensure we successfully support companies and knowledge workers in this new world of work. 

We embrace asynchronous communication and collaborative work and share our practices in the Oyster Public HQ https://oysterhr.notion.site/Welcome-to-The-Reef-Oyster-s-Open-Source-Employee-Guide-fc482e3f107c4afd8c85ed217f1d0c1d to help other global teams learn from our experiences.


HOW YOU WORK

With diverse locations, cultures, and needs, we created How YOU Work; https://oysterhr.notion.site/How-YOU-work-ac16c1932e314ffd8cb693e332458395 a program supporting your whole human experience at Oyster:

 - Work from anywhere: Oyster has no borders or HQ. As long as work is timely, your team is supported, and you're authorized to work where you live, you can work from anywhere.

 - Paid time off: Enjoy 40 days off per year (including holidays and vacation), or more if required by your country.

 - Mental health support: Access Plumm https://oysterhr.notion.site/Plumm-3b5e7e70dec6428e975f68f25972cfb7, our mental well-being service.

 - Wellbeing allowance: Each month, receive a wellbeing allowance in your ThanksBen wallet. Spend it on a wide range of options; see the benefits catalogue https://www.thanksben.com/employee-benefits-catalogue for ideas.

 - Flexible parental leave: All new parents are eligible for at least three months’ paid leave, with job protection for up to 12 months or as required locally.

 - WFH stipend: Receive a stipend for your laptop and home office equipment to get you set up quickly.


✨ THE BEST JOBS SHOULD BE AVAILABLE TO EVERYONE 

We embrace diverse perspectives and intentionally build our product and company with an inclusive, global mindset. We encourage you to apply, even if you don’t meet every criterion, and are committed to a trust-based, thriving environment.

Oyster is committed to ensuring equal opportunity of employment for qualified persons with disabilities and actively fosters an inclusive work environment. If you require reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process, please email jobs@oysterhr.com with the subject line: Interview Accommodations.

Our available positions are on our careers page. Our team will only contact you from an @oysterhr.com email, and we will never ask for money as part of an interview process or job offer. If you receive a suspicious email about Oyster jobs or are directed to a site other than www.oysterhr.com/careers http://www.oysterhr.com/careers, please report it via our Compliance and Ethics Helpline https://www.oysterhr.com/trust.
fields.title: Senior Manager, People Programs
